steps.title: Spot the cause-and-effect relationship
steps.content: The first sentence establishes that the graphics lack organic realism because they're built from perfect geometric shapes. The second sentence describes what this deficiency looks like: unnaturally precise angles and curves. The transition must connect these as cause and consequence.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content: ”As such” expresses a consequence—it directly shows that the unnaturally precise angles and curves result from the graphics lacking organic realism. The other three fail to capture this relationship: “In addition” treats it as separate information, “In conclusion” wrongly implies finality, and “However” introduces a non-existent contrast. This transition completes the logical chain from the problem (lack of realism) to its visible manifestation (precise angles and curves). That matches choice A — A is correct.
